# build123d insane: root package multi-format importers

Wrap the `build123d` package root while filtering down to five real importer routes across STL, SVG, STEP, BREP, and SVG-to-code workflows.

## Why This Case Exists

The top build123d rung should start from the broad package root and still retain a useful multi-format importer slice without manual cleanup.

## Case Shape

- ID: `build123d_root`
- Family: `build123d`
- Difficulty: `insane`
- Tier: `local_full`
- Style: `package`
- Workflow Slice: `manufacturing`
- Target Kind: `package`
- Upstream Target: `build123d`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/build123d_root/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

- Minimum generated tools: `5`
- Required generated tools: `importers__import_brep`, `importers__import_step`, `importers__import_stl`, `importers__import_svg`, `importers__import_svg_as_buildline_code`

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the real-world example lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=build123d_root
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only build123d_root
```

## Prepared Inputs

This case does not need rendered setup inputs.

## Fixtures

- `challenges/cases/build123d_root/fixtures/rect.brep`
- `challenges/cases/build123d_root/fixtures/rect.step`
- `challenges/cases/build123d_root/fixtures/tri.stl`
- `challenges/cases/build123d_root/fixtures/tri.svg`

## Workflow

### 1. Import the STL fixture through the filtered root facade

Tool: `importers__import_stl`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "file_name": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/tri.stl"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['Face object'].

### 2. Import the SVG fixture through the filtered root facade

Tool: `importers__import_svg`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "svg_file": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/tri.svg"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['Face object'].

### 3. Convert the SVG fixture into build123d BuildLine code through the root facade

Tool: `importers__import_svg_as_buildline_code`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "file_name": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/tri.svg"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['BuildLine', 'Line('].

### 4. Import the STEP fixture through the filtered root facade

Tool: `importers__import_step`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "filename": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/rect.step"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['Shell object'].

### 5. Import the BREP fixture through the filtered root facade

Tool: `importers__import_brep`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "file_name": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/rect.brep"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['Compound'].

## What This Case Proves

- Package-root ingestion can be constrained with module and symbol filters while still keeping multiple useful routes.
- The filtered wrapper still executes real upstream geometry imports from more than one file format.
- The challenge stays inspectable even when the original namespace is broad.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: start from the broad package root, then deterministically narrow down.
# This proves the scaffold filters can carve a useful example out of a bigger namespace.
id = "build123d_root"
title = "build123d insane: root package multi-format importers"
tier = "local_full"
style = "package"
slice = "manufacturing"
family = "build123d"
difficulty = "insane"

[example]
summary = "Wrap the `build123d` package root while filtering down to five real importer routes across STL, SVG, STEP, BREP, and SVG-to-code workflows."
motivation = "The top build123d rung should start from the broad package root and still retain a useful multi-format importer slice without manual cleanup."
proves = [
  "Package-root ingestion can be constrained with module and symbol filters while still keeping multiple useful routes.",
  "The filtered wrapper still executes real upstream geometry imports from more than one file format.",
  "The challenge stays inspectable even when the original namespace is broad.",
]

[target]
kind = "package"
value = "build123d"

[probe]
imports = ["build123d"]

[scaffold]
kind = "package"
include_submodules = true
module_include_patterns = ["^build123d\\.importers$"]
symbol_include_patterns = [
  "^import_brep$",
  "^import_step$",
  "^import_stl$",
  "^import_svg$",
  "^import_svg_as_buildline_code$",
]
max_generated_tools = 12

[ingestion]
min_generated_tools = 5
required_tools = [
  "importers__import_brep",
  "importers__import_step",
  "importers__import_stl",
  "importers__import_svg",
  "importers__import_svg_as_buildline_code",
]

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Import the STL fixture through the filtered root facade"
tool = "importers__import_stl"
arguments = { file_name = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/tri.stl" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["Face object"]

[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Import the SVG fixture through the filtered root facade"
tool = "importers__import_svg"
arguments = { svg_file = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/tri.svg" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["Face object"]

[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Convert the SVG fixture into build123d BuildLine code through the root facade"
tool = "importers__import_svg_as_buildline_code"
arguments = { file_name = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/tri.svg" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["BuildLine", "Line("]

[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Import the STEP fixture through the filtered root facade"
tool = "importers__import_step"
arguments = { filename = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/rect.step" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["Shell object"]

[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Import the BREP fixture through the filtered root facade"
tool = "importers__import_brep"
arguments = { file_name = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/rect.brep" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["Compound"]
```
